highlights.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Executives Plan to Attend G7 Summit
highlights.description: Artificial intelligence executives from Anthropic PBC, OpenAI and Alphabet Inc.’s Google are slated to attend the Group of Seven summit, a gathering of world leaders that gets underway next week in France.

highlights.title: SpaceX Makes History With Biggest-Ever IPO
highlights.description: SpaceX raised $75 billion in its IPO, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 each, sending it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matthew Bloxham breaks down the situation and what to expect from SpaceX's first day of trading. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Scammers Used Gemini AI to Help Build Spam Messages, Google Says
highlights.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google filed a lawsuit against a suspected Chinese cybercrime operation, alleging that the group used artificial intelligence to send more than 2 million text messages with fraudulent links attempting to scam cellphone users.

highlights.title: SpaceX Shares Indicated More Than 35% Higher on Gray Markets
highlights.description: Pre-IPO trading in derivatives linked to SpaceX indicates a gain of anything between 30% and 50% for Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company as retail investors flock to the much anticipated listing.

highlights.title: SpaceX’s $100 Billion Order Book Is Tiny Versus Hot Chinese IPOs
highlights.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX made history with the biggest initial public offering of all time, but there’s at least one metric where the deal falls short.

highlights.title: SpaceX’s Gwynne Shotwell had IPO doubts for years, now she has a message for investors
highlights.description: Gwynne Shotwell, long Elon Musk's second-in-command at SpaceX, spoke exclusively with CNBC ahead of her company's highly anticipated IPO.

highlights.title: RLRWLD CEO on Nvidia Partnership, DexBench
highlights.description:
Junghee Ryu, Founder and CEO of South Korean robotics startup, RLWRLD, discusses the company's partnership with Nvidia to build a universal benchmark for evaluating how well robots use their hands to manipulate objects and complete tasks. The company is aiming to develop next-generation industry standards for humanoid robotics.
He speaks with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: SpaceX IPO Puts Elon Musk’s ‘Extreme’ Ownership to the Test
highlights.description: The rocket maker debuts on Nasdaq today under a wave of criticism about Musk’s near-absolute control. It’s how the company has worked from the start.

highlights.title: Siri won’t be your AI girlfriend
highlights.description:
‘Listen, that's not what I'm here for.' | Image: Apple	

Our early testing has already shown that Siri AI knows when to shut up, and that's very much by design. In an interview with Mostly Human spotted by MacRumors, Craig Federighi said Apple's new Siri won't act all sycophantic like chatbots made by OpenAI, Google, and others.
"As you may know, if you use many of the existing chatbots, they're really focused on engagement to a large degree," said Federighi, who is responsible for software at Apple. "And sycophancy, right? They kind of want to pull you in. They might encourage you to reveal things about yourself, and then use that as a basis to establish a connection."

Apple purposely took a di …
Read the full story at The Verge.

highlights.title: SpaceX raising $75 billion in record-setting IPO as Nasdaq debut awaits
highlights.description: SpaceX is selling 555.6 shares at $135 a piece, raising $75 billion in the largest IPO on record.

ai.title: Apple’s new Foundation Models explained: on-device AI, cloud AI, and everything in between
ai.description: During the WWDC26 keynote, Apple announced its third generation of Apple Foundation Models (AFM), comprising five models, some of which are local, some of which are cloud-based, and one of which lives in Google’s servers running on Nvidia chips. Here’s a breakdown of how that will work.

ai.title: SpaceX IPO Shows Willingness to Finance AI, Waldron Says
ai.description: John Waldron, Goldman Sachs president and chief operating officer discusses SpaceX's record IPO and whether markets can absorb two other possible big IPOs by the end of the year. "Capital markets are demonstrating a willingness to finance these extraordinary companies as we build out this AI infrastructure," he tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: You Probably Won’t Get Rich Off the SpaceX IPO
ai.description: The company has set aside an unusually high number of shares for retail investors. Still, experts say, you’re just getting the crumbs.

ai.title: Goldman’s Waldron Says SpaceX Shows Investor Zeal for Funding AI
ai.description: SpaceX’s record-setting initial public offering shows investors are eager to finance artificial intelligence infrastructure, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. President John Waldron.

ai.title: Odd Lots: Making the Hay Market More Transparent (Podcast)
ai.description: The hay market is not a transparent market: It is very fractured by types of hay, whether it is alfalfa or clover hay. There are a few opaque, illiquid markets like this — scrap metal for instance — that require some hands-on investigating to figure out. Aiden Johnson is co-founder and CEO of the HayWire newsletter, which aims to make the hay market more transparent: He and co-founder Cole Glasgow use an AI model to mine public data sources — like USDA reports on auction prices across different

ai.title: Banks Curb Hedge Fund Bets on SK Hynix, Samsung After Rally
ai.description: Global banks are curbing hedge funds’ leveraged bets on Asia’s top chipmakers including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co. after a blistering rally this year raised concerns of a potential pullback, according to people familiar with the matter.

ai.title: ChatGPT hits a billion monthly app users despite souring public AI sentiment
ai.description: ChatGPT reached a billion monthly users in May despite growing unease over its ethical and environmental impacts.

ai.title: Kioxia Becomes Japan’s Most Valuable Firm as AI Mania Goes On
ai.description: Memory chipmaker Kioxia Holdings Corp. replaced Toyota Motor Corp. to become Japan’s largest company by market value, underscoring how the global artificial intelligence boom is reshaping the country’s corporate landscape.

ai.title: Chinese Robot Appliance Maker Dreame Tech Is Said to Consider IPO in Hong Kong
ai.description: Dreame Technology, a Chinese maker of robotic vacuum cleaners and other smart home appliances, is considering a Hong Kong initial public offering as soon as next year, people familiar with the matter said.

ai.title: Apple’s Camera Chief Thinks AI Can Give You Superpowers
ai.description: The generative features in iOS 27’s new Photos app will add fake pixels to some of your shots, but Apple’s Jon McCormack says the company isn’t using AI “for the sake of AI.”

ai.title: London's AI Boom | Bloomberg Tech: Europe 6/12/2026
ai.description:
In this episode of Bloomberg Tech: Europe, Bloomberg’s Tom Mackenzie dives into London's AI boom. The UK capital is on a tear, with Britain leading the AI wave in Europe, so what's behind the explosive growth and how did London become a global center for artificial intelligence? Bloomberg speaks to key players, including the co-Founder of ElevenLabs - the UK's most valuable AI start-up.

"Bloomberg Tech: Europe" spotlights the biggest names and trends shaping the region's technology ecosystem as the global competition heats up. This monthly, 30-minute magazine-style show features in-depth interviews with top technology leaders, as well as major investors and policymakers - giving you a compelling A to Z of the most consequential innovations, opportunities and challenges.  (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Tech Secretary Vows More Help for UK AI Sector
ai.description: Technology Secretary Liz Kendall says the UK’s Labour government will strengthen efforts to drive institutional funds into British tech companies, as she pledged more backing for the country’s artificial intelligence sector. Speaking to Bloomberg TV during London Tech Week, Kendall said she wants The government wants to unlock investment into UK firms with legal reforms to how pension funds are run. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: UK Needs More Affordable Power Plants To Drive AI: Miller
ai.description:
Evantic Capital Founder and Managing Partner Matt Miller says the UK needs to invest in more affordable power plants, including nuclear energy, to boost the the country's AI sector.
Miller is a former partner at Sequoia Capital who last year founded Evantic, a 400 million dollar UK-based venture capital fund. Miller spoke to Tom Mackenzie on Bloomberg Tech: Europe. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: UK Tech Secretary Vows More Action on Pushing Funds to Startups
ai.description: The UK’s Labour government will strengthen efforts to drive institutional funds into British tech companies, Technology Secretary Liz Kendall said, as she pledged more backing for the country’s artificial intelligence sector.

ai.title: 9to5Mac Daily: June 11, 2026 – visionOS 27 features, more
ai.description:
Listen to a recap of the top stories of the day from 9to5Mac. 9to5Mac Daily is available on iTunes and Apple’s Podcasts app, Stitcher, TuneIn, Google Play, or through our dedicated RSS feed for Overcast and other podcast players.
Sponsored by CardPointers: The best way to maximize your credit card rewards. 9to5Mac Daily listeners can exclusively save 30% and get a $200 Savings Card.

ai.title: Matchmakers Are Being Paid $25K to Find Trad Wives for Rich Men
ai.description: Even in blue states, nonreligious tech entrepreneurs and CEOs are increasingly asking for “traditional” and “conservative” women, matchmakers tell WIRED.

ai.title: The Next US-China AI Battle Is Over Compute — and China Is Spending Big
ai.description: China bets $295 billion on AI backbone as US data center boom accelerates

business.title: SpaceX Makes History With Biggest-Ever IPO
business.description: SpaceX raised $75 billion in its IPO, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 each, sending it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matthew Bloxham breaks down the situation and what to expect from SpaceX's first day of trading. (Source: Bloomberg)

business.title: SpaceX’s $100 Billion Order Book Is Tiny Versus Hot Chinese IPOs
business.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX made history with the biggest initial public offering of all time, but there’s at least one metric where the deal falls short.

business.title: SpaceX raising $75 billion in record-setting IPO as Nasdaq debut awaits
business.description: SpaceX is selling 555.6 shares at $135 a piece, raising $75 billion in the largest IPO on record.

business.title: SpaceX Prepares for Debut as $75 Billion IPO Breaks Record
business.description: SpaceX made history with a $75 billion IPO that instantly turned it into one of the biggest public companies in the world. Now it has to win over the market.

business.title: SpaceX Raises $2.2 Billion in Japan Slice of Record-Breaking IPO
business.description: SpaceX raised $2.2 billion from Japanese investors, according to a regulatory filing on Friday, as part of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering.

business.title: SpaceX's crypto-traded IPO was sharply falling. It now points upward to a $2.4 trillion valuation
business.description: The SPCX perpetual on Hyperliquid has bounced from this week’s lows, while Bloomberg says other shadow markets now imply a first-day gain of more than 35%.

business.title: SpaceX Governance Standards Too Weak: Danish Pension CIO
business.description: Anders Schelde, chief investment officer of AkademikerPension, a $25 billion Danish pension fund, explains his concerns about SpaceX's valuation and governance structure. Speaking from Copenhagen on Bloomberg Television, Schelde says the risk of a sharp repricing is "definitely on the table." (Source: Bloomberg)

space.title: SpaceX Shares Indicated More Than 35% Higher on Gray Markets
space.description: Pre-IPO trading in derivatives linked to SpaceX indicates a gain of anything between 30% and 50% for Elon Musk’s rocket, satellite and AI company as retail investors flock to the much anticipated listing.

space.title: SpaceX’s Gwynne Shotwell had IPO doubts for years, now she has a message for investors
space.description: Gwynne Shotwell, long Elon Musk's second-in-command at SpaceX, spoke exclusively with CNBC ahead of her company's highly anticipated IPO.

space.title: SpaceX IPO Puts Elon Musk’s ‘Extreme’ Ownership to the Test
space.description: The rocket maker debuts on Nasdaq today under a wave of criticism about Musk’s near-absolute control. It’s how the company has worked from the start.

Junghee Ryu, Founder and CEO of South Korean robotics startup, RLWRLD, discusses the company's partnership with Nvidia to build a universal benchmark for evaluating how well robots use their hands to manipulate objects and complete tasks. The company is aiming to develop next-generation industry standards for humanoid robotics.
He speaks with Shery Ahn on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

robotics.title: Humanoid Robot Manufacturer EngineAI Is Said to File for Hong Kong IPO
robotics.description: Chinese robotics startup EngineAI has filed confidentially for a Hong Kong initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, joining a legion of companies in the sector seeking funding.

robotics.title: Theker just raised $85M to build the factory robot that doesn’t specialize in anything
robotics.description: Unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form — think Boston Dynamics — Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured.

robotics.title: Do Humanoid Robots Really Need Legs?
robotics.description: There’s a long-running disagreement among roboticists about whether wheeled robots make more sense than walking ones.

ev_autonomous.title: Metaplanet buys Siiibo Securities to accelerate bitcoin financial ecosystem plans
ev_autonomous.description: The approximately $13.1 million acquisition gives Metaplanet a regulated securities platform to develop Bitcoin-linked investment products.

regulation.title: Former SEC, CFTC Chair Gary Gensler argues that prediction markets don't overrule state regulations
regulation.description: The former regulator joined a number of interest groups in arguing that prediction markets are overstepping their bounds by offering sports-related contracts.

regulation.title: Infineon to Open German Chip Fab as Part of EU Sovereignty Push
regulation.description: Infineon Technologies AG is preparing to open its largest single investment, a €5 billion ($5.8 billion) semiconductor factory built with the help of European Union subsidies, as the bloc seeks to boost chip production.

chips_cloud.title: Delos Data offers AI chip startups a fast track to rack scale
chips_cloud.description: Half the trouble of building an Nvidia NVL or AMD Helios competitor is just getting the networking out of the box

chips_cloud.title: AMD denies researcher a $10,000 bug bounty after fixing critical auto-updater vulnerability — security flaw took 124 days to patch
chips_cloud.description: AMD took over four months to fix a critical security bug in its autoupdater, and the security researcher didn't see a dime for his efforts

crypto.title: Live updates: Bitcoin traders eye rally to $75,000, Japan set to hike rate to 31-year high
crypto.description: Crypto firmed across the board following the largest equity listing in history. The token most tied to Elon Musk traded right in line with the market.
